description: Papers on step detection algorithms, activity recognition, step count ML, demographic bias, device comparison, wearable accuracy, energy expenditure estimation, and ground truth validation (2020-2026).

papers.label: Validation of Inertial Sensor-Based Step Detection Algorithms for Edge Device Deployment
papers.authors: Kisiel M, Amjad A, Szczęsna A
papers.headline: Validates and compares five fundamentally different step detection algorithms using IMU data, with a focus on deployment to edge devices.

papers.label: A Transparent Method for Step Detection using an Acceleration Threshold
papers.authors: Ducharme SW, Lim J, Busa MA, Aguiar EJ, Moore CC, Schuna JM Jr, Barreira TV, Staudenmayer J, Chipkin SR, Tudor-Locke C
papers.venue: Journal for the Measurement of Physical Behaviour
papers.headline: Develops and validates a simple, transparent acceleration threshold-based step detection algorithm to counter proprietary black-box algorithms in consumer devices.

papers.label: Open-Source, Step-Counting Algorithm for Smartphone Data Collected in Clinical and Nonclinical Settings
papers.authors: Straczkiewicz M, Keating NL, Thompson E, Matulonis UA, Campos SM, Wright AA, Onnela JP
papers.headline: Develops and validates an open-source step-counting algorithm for smartphone accelerometer data in clinical (oncology) and nonclinical settings.

papers.label: Real-World Gait Detection Using a Wrist-Worn Inertial Sensor: Validation Study
papers.authors: Kluge F, Brand YE, Micó-Amigo ME, et al.
papers.headline: Validates wrist-worn inertial sensor algorithms for detecting gait episodes prior to spatiotemporal parameter estimation in real-world settings.

papers.label: DeM-FCN: An Ultra-Lightweight and Purely Convolutional Framework for Edge-Native Human Activity Recognition
papers.authors: Xu Y, Li J, Huang Z, Cheng T, Chen C, Tan B, Tan Z, Chen H, Zhou Y
papers.venue: Frontiers in Sports and Active Living
papers.headline: Proposes DeM-FCN, an ultra-lightweight fully convolutional network for on-device human activity recognition targeting wearable fitness trackers with limited compute.

papers.label: An Open-Source, Open Data Approach to Activity Classification from Triaxial Accelerometry in an Ambulatory Setting
papers.authors: Nikookar S, Tian E, Hoffman H, Parks M, McKay JL, Kiarashi Y, Thomas TT, Hall A, Wright DW, Clifford GD
papers.headline: Develops an open dataset and open-source code for activity classification from triaxial accelerometry, moving beyond step counting toward richer activity recognition.

papers.label: Continuous Assessment of Daily-Living Gait Using Self-Supervised Learning of Wrist-Worn Accelerometer Data
papers.authors: Brand YE, Buchman AS, Kluge F, Palmerini L, Becker C, Cereatti A, Maetzler W, Vereijken B, Yarnall AJ, Rochester L, Del Din S, Mueller A, Hausdorff JM, Perlman O
papers.headline: Applies self-supervised learning to wrist-worn accelerometer data for continuous gait assessment in daily living without labeled training data.

papers.label: A Wearable IMU-Based Framework for Daily Physical Activity Recognition and Energy Expenditure Level Classification
papers.authors: Zhou X, Wang S, He L, Ma L
papers.headline: Develops an IMU-based framework combining activity recognition with energy expenditure level classification for daily PA monitoring.

papers.label: Application of Machine Learning Approach to Classify Human Activity Level Based on Lifelog Data
papers.authors: Jeong S, Nam W, Park K
papers.headline: Applies machine learning to classify human activity levels from lifelog data including step counts, heart rate, and contextual sensors.

papers.label: DaRA Dataset: Combining Wearable Sensors, Location Tracking, and Process Knowledge for Human Activity Recognition in Warehousing
papers.authors: Niemann F, Rueda FM, Al Kfari MK, Nair NR, Schauten D, Kretschmer V, Lüdtke S, Kirchheim A
papers.headline: Presents a multimodal dataset combining wearable sensors, location tracking, and process knowledge for HAR in industrial warehousing.

papers.label: Why Your Smartwatch May Be Misleading Your Doctor: Mobility Aid Impact on Wearable Accuracy in Older Adults
papers.authors: Özel A, Yakşi E, Çevik Ç, Nazar MR
papers.headline: Investigates how mobility aids (walkers, canes) impact smartwatch step count accuracy in older adults. A negative-result study highlighting device limitations.

papers.label: Comparison of Consumer-Grade Wearable Devices with a Research-Grade Instrument for Measuring PA in Free-Living
papers.authors: Miwa T, Mii K, Chatani R, Sugitani Y
papers.headline: Compares multiple consumer-grade wearables against a research-grade accelerometer for step count and PA measurement in free-living conditions.

papers.label: Accurate Measurement of Simulated Slow and Altered Walking Activity: Apple Watch Best in Class
papers.authors: Rowe G, Weight D, Rea A, Conlon J, Wood FM, Edgar DW
papers.headline: Evaluates multiple consumer wearables for step count accuracy during simulated slow and altered walking patterns relevant for clinical populations.

papers.label: Accuracy of Apple Watch and Fitbit for Assessing EE and HR of Wheelchair Users
papers.authors: Danielsson ML, Vergeer M, Plasqui G, Baumgart JK
papers.headline: Evaluates smartwatch accuracy for EE and HR in wheelchair users. Critical negative-result study for an understudied population.

papers.label: Low-Cost Consumer-Based Trackers to Measure PA and Sleep in Free-Living: Validation Study
papers.authors: Degroote L, Hamerlinck G, Poels K, Maher C, Crombez G, De Bourdeaudhuij I, Vandendriessche A, Curtis RG, DeSmet A
papers.headline: Validates low-cost consumer activity trackers against research-grade accelerometers for step count and sleep in free-living conditions.

papers.label: Step Count Accuracy of Life Plus Connected Watch at Different Locations and Speeds in Healthy, CVD, and PAD Patients
papers.authors: Heizmann AN, Ollier E, Labeix P, Goujon I, Roche F, Le Hello C
papers.headline: Evaluates step count accuracy at different body locations and walking speeds in healthy adults, CVD patients, and PAD patients.

papers.label: Step-Counting Accuracy of a Commercial Smartwatch in Mild-to-Moderate PD Patients
papers.authors: Bianchini E, Caliò B, Alborghetti M, Rinaldi D, Hansen C, Vuillerme N, Maetzler W, Pontieri FE
papers.headline: Assesses smartwatch step-counting accuracy in PD, examining effects of gait parameters, symptom laterality, and medication state.

papers.label: Accuracy of Huawei GT2 Smartwatch for Measuring PA and Sleep During Daily Life
papers.authors: Mei L, He Z, Hu L
papers.headline: Validates Huawei GT2 smartwatch step count and sleep against research-grade devices in free-living conditions.

papers.label: From Movement to METs: Validation of ActTrust for EE Estimation and PA Classification
papers.authors: Dos Santos Batista E, Basilio Silva Gomes SR, de Morais Ferreira AB, França LGS, Fontenele Araújo J, Mortatti AL, Leocadio-Miguel MA
papers.headline: Validates ActTrust accelerometer for EE estimation and PA intensity classification using indirect calorimetry as criterion.

papers.label: OpenMetabolics: Estimating EE Using a Smartphone Worn in a Pocket
papers.authors: Cho H, Slade P
papers.headline: Presents OpenMetabolics, an open-source method for estimating EE using smartphone IMU data from a pocket placement.

papers.label: Estimation of Walking EE Using a Single Sacrum-Mounted IMU Based on Biomechanically-Informed ML
papers.authors: Jung J, Lim H, Jeong H, Upadhye S, Kim JH, Park S
papers.headline: Combines biomechanical principles with ML to estimate walking EE from a single sacrum-mounted IMU.

papers.label: Reducing Bias and Enhancing Equity in AI-Enabled Precision Nutrition: Wearable Measurement Error
papers.authors: Mai A, Luan Y, Loy SL, Qin C, Ji H, Obeng A, Oladuti M, Ejima K, et al.
papers.headline: Review examining how measurement error in wearables (including step count) varies across demographic groups and contributes to bias in AI health models.

papers.label: Digital Health Interventions for Lifestyle Behaviors: Who Is Being Left Behind? Social Inequality Evidence Synthesis
papers.authors: Jiang J, Zhong Q, Yin Z, Zhou Q, Wang Y, Yan Z
papers.venue: Int J Behav Nutr Phys Act
papers.headline: Systematic review examining social inequalities in digital health interventions targeting lifestyle behaviors including step count monitoring.

papers.label: OxWEARS: Free-Living Ground-Truth Validation Dataset for Wearable Measures of PA, Sleep, and HR
papers.authors: Maylor BD, Small SR, Plekhanova T, Brocklebank L, van Duijvenboden S, Sharman R, Hill EA, Karpe F, Kyle SD, Doherty A
papers.headline: Protocol generating a comprehensive free-living ground-truth dataset with body camera annotation, ankle accelerometer for steps, and polysomnography.

papers.label: Analytical and Clinical Validation of Step Counting in People Living with Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis
papers.authors: Straczkiewicz M, Burke KM, Carney KT, Calcagno N, Mandepudi S, Premasiri A, Vieira FG, Berry JD
papers.headline: Validates step counting methods in people living with ALS, a population with highly variable and impaired gait patterns.
